2 hours ago
"A story is not what happened. A story is what the listener takes home."
— Dr. Nomsa Mdlalose · Module 1
247 likes
drsasa.zintsomi The listener is not passive. They are co-creator. This is what Module 1 teaches — #Zintsomi #Storytelling #AfricanStories
T
thandi_k This changed how I teach. Every lesson starts with a story now 🙌
1h ago

sipho_m The co-creator concept is profound. Fire 🔥

nomvula_d I'm an attorney and this course transformed my closing arguments. Thank you Dr Sasa ✨

drsasa.zintsomi
3 days ago
"The night my grandmother told me the story that changed everything"
Full blog post →
1,247 likes
drsasa.zintsomi She didn't start with "once upon a time." She started with silence. And in that silence, we all leaned in. This is the full story I've been meaning to write for years. #Grandmother #Ubuntu #Roots
K
kagiso_t I cried reading this. My ouma told stories the same way. Starting with silence 🕊️
1d ago
A
ayanda_n The silence IS the story. That's exactly what Module 3 taught me. 🙏
22h ago

Bring Your Story to the Fire

Upload any MP3 — a folktale, a lullaby, a proverb, a lecture. It plays here in the village for everyone around the fire to hear.

Story Library

Choose Your Story

Each pod is a full 360° VR environment. Wear your headset or explore on any screen.

isiZulu · English
The Night the Ancestors Spoke
A grandmother calls her grandchildren to the fire. What she tells them will change how they see the world.
12 min
Storytelling Fire
isiXhosa · English
Umthi Wezinyanya — The Ancestor Tree
Stand beneath the ancient baobab as the elders pass down the names of those who came before.
9 min
Sacred Grove
Sesotho · English
Lullaby of the Moon Mothers
Lie back under a virtual African sky as traditional lullabies are sung in the language of the stars.
8 min
Night Sky Dome
West Africa · Drum Language
The Djembe That Spoke Truth
A master drummer discovers that his drum carries messages from the spirit world. The village must listen.
15 min
Village Square
Tshivenda · English
Why the Crocodile Lives in Water
A classic izinganekwane — why did the crocodile choose the river? The answer will surprise you.
7 min
River Bank
Setswana · English
The Chief Who Forgot to Listen
A powerful parable about leadership, humility, and what happens when a ruler stops hearing his people.
11 min
Royal Kraal
Dr Sasa · Exclusive
The Story That Built Zintsomi
Dr Nomsa Sasa Mdlalose tells the story of how she founded the college — told as a story, not a speech.
18 min
Dr Sasa\'s Study
Community · All Languages

How to Use VR Mode
1. Open on Phone
Open Zintsomi in any mobile browser (Chrome recommended)
▶
2. Press Play
Tap any story pod and press the VR play button
3. Insert in Headset
Place phone in Google Cardboard or compatible headset
4. Listen & Explore
Look around the 360° village as the story plays around you

Phone + Cardboard
1. Open on your phone
2. Choose a story
3. Tap 🥽 VR Split mode
4. Insert phone into Google Cardboard
5. Look around — tilt to explore
🥽
Meta Quest / WebXR
1. Open site in Quest Browser
2. Choose a story
3. Tap 🥽 VR Split
4. Enter immersive mode
5. Use controllers to turn pages
💻
Desktop / Laptop
1. Click 🎬 Cinema mode
2. Fullscreen for best effect
3. Drag the scene to rotate
4. Read the story alongside

🪄 Izibongo ya Gama Lakho
Type your name. The AI composes your personal izibongo — a praise poem in isiZulu — in the tradition of the ancestors. First in Africa.
isiZulu Praise Poetry — Izibongo

Izibongo is the ancient isiZulu art of praise poetry — recited at births, ceremonies, and moments of honour. This feature uses AI trained in isiZulu oral tradition to compose yours.

African Traditional Games

Izingedo · Inqathu · Umlabalaba — African tradition, digital play

Play & Learn

Choose Your Game

Each game rooted in African tradition. Read the teaser, then dive in and play.

🪨🪨🪨
tap the rhythm
Rhythm
Izingedo / Diketo
🇿🇦 Sotho tradition · South Africa

Watch the pattern flash. Tap it back in exact order. Faster every round. Deeply addictive.

20 Levels
↑ Speed
★ Scoring
🪢
read pattern · jump in time
Pattern Logic
Inqathu / Kgati
🇿🇦 Sotho & Tswana tradition

The digital rope never stops. Read the jump sequence. Input it before the rope catches you.

15 Levels
⚡ Speed+
3 Lives
Memory
Umlabalaba / Ncuva
🇿🇦 Venda tradition · Limpopo

Patterns flash. Memorise them. Recreate perfectly. Each round grows more complex. Trains focus and visual memory.

12 Levels
Memory
+ Complexity
Family Games

Table Games for Everyone

A shared family corner: 100 quiz questions with answers, classic solitaire, snakes & ladders, chess, and checkers. Built for local play first; multiplayer/cloud leaderboards can come later.

100 Questions
Family Quiz Bowl
Storytelling · Culture · Science · Maths

Real multiple-choice questions with instant answers and explanations. Rotate through 100 prompts.

Solo Logic
Peg Solitaire
Classic board puzzle

Jump pegs into empty spaces until only one remains. Simple rules, deep strategy.

Dice Race
Snakes & Ladders
Family tabletop classic

Roll the dice, climb ladders, avoid snakes, and race to square 100.

Strategy
Chess
Local two-player board

Play a real chess board with legal piece movement, captures, and pawn promotion.

Tactics
Checkers
Local two-player board

Diagonal moves, captures, and king promotion. A fast tactical family match.

🫘
Ancient Strategy
Mancala
🌍 African · 2 players · Ages 6+

One of the oldest board games on earth. Sow seeds around the board, capture your opponent's pits, and fill your store to win.

🔥
Storytelling
Ubuntu Story Chain
🌍 African oral tradition · 2–4 players

Pass-and-tell family storytelling. One sentence at a time. Read the full story back at the end.

Family Game
🪨
